summary: PR #2263 decompiled six stage functions across grcastle, grcorneria, grrcruise, grzebesroute, groldyoshi, and grpushon, updated several recovered prototypes in headers, and added or refined small local stage data/ground-var structs needed by those matches. The PR body claims ninja builds clean and 100.0 fuzzy match for all six functions; the bot report corroborates six new 100% matches totaling +680 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Recovered fn_801CFB68 and adjusted grCastleParams x4 from f32 into two s16 fields.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototype for fn_801CFB68 with void fn_801CFB68(Item_GObj*, Ground*, HSD_GObj*) and added it/forward.h.
Recovered fn_801E12D4 beam-damage handler, added static grCn_804D69A0, uses OSReport, damage accumulation, M2C_FIELD threshold access, and cannon material calls.
Recovered fn_80201BE0 display gate using gm_801A45E8, Camera_8003010C, and grDisplay_801C5DB0; added required includes.
Updated fn_80201BE0 prototype from unknown to void fn_80201BE0(HSD_GObj*, s32).
Recovered fn_8020B4D8 collision/stage flag handler with PAD_STACK(16).
Updated fn_8020B4D8 prototype and added lb/mp forward includes.
Recovered fn_8020F2A8 collision/joint lookup handler, added local grOy_JointPair typedef and extern grOy_803E6574.
Updated fn_8020F2A8 prototype and added forward includes needed by recovered signature.
Recovered grPushOn_80219230 sentinel lookup function and refined grPushOn data layout by splitting x1c and adding x10c lookup entries.
Added grOldYoshi_Entry and grOldYoshi_GroundVars, then added oldyoshi union arm to Ground for typed access in fn_8020F2A8.

what_changed: Decompiled six previously placeholder stage functions: fn_801CFB68, fn_801E12D4, fn_80201BE0, fn_8020B4D8, fn_8020F2A8, and grPushOn_80219230.
Updated recovered function prototypes in grcastle.h, grrcruise.h, grzebesroute.h, and groldyoshi.h, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS where signatures were recovered.
Added includes or forward declarations needed by recovered signatures and calls, including it/forward.h, lb/forward.h, mp/forward.h, cm/camera.h, gm/gm_1A45.h, and gr/grdisplay.h.
Refined small stage data layouts: grCastleParams x4 became s16 x4 plus s16 x6; grPushOn_804D6AB8 gained a lookup-table struct; Ground gained a grOldYoshi_GroundVars union arm.
Introduced local typed access for Old Yoshi ground vars and joint-pair data instead of leaving only placeholder function body.
Kept at least one bridge-style access in grcorneria via M2C_FIELD(grCn_804D69A0, f32*, 0x38), indicating the threshold data type was not fully recovered in this PR.
smart_moves: Header prototypes were updated alongside recovered function bodies, keeping declarations consistent with new signatures.
The Old Yoshi function used a recovered Ground union arm and bitfield entry structure to express gp->gv.oldyoshi.entries[idx].b4 instead of raw offset writes.
The grpushon match modeled a key/value lookup table with a small struct and a sentinel loop, making the recovered function more reviewable than raw array indexing.
The PR limited the scope to six small stage functions and their immediate type/prototype dependencies, making the matching changes easy to audit.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered stage callback proves its parameter types, update the owning header prototype rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.
Small stage-specific GroundVars structs can turn callback body writes into typed union-arm accesses, as shown by gp->gv.oldyoshi.entries[idx].b4 in fn_8020F2A8.
For table search functions, model the backing data as a key/value struct and recover the sentinel loop when the function scans until key == -1.
A 100% function match can still contain bridge code such as M2C_FIELD or PAD_STACK; preserve those as narrow tactics only when the PR evidence says they were part of the matched solution.

naming_conventions.convention: Preserved address-style fn_ names for functions whose semantic names were not proven: fn_801CFB68, fn_801E12D4, fn_80201BE0, fn_8020B4D8, and fn_8020F2A8.
Stage-local globals and structs followed existing stage prefixes: grCs_ for Castle, grCn_ for Corneria, grOy_ for Old Yoshi, and grPushOn_ for PushOn.
Temporary or unknown fields retained offset-style names such as x4, x6, x10c, x114, and x108 where stronger semantics were not provided by the PR evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8020B4D8 to obtain the matched grzebesroute function shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: PAD_STACK is a matching tactic and should remain narrow; no adjacent-function regression detail is present beyond the bot's new-match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recovered grPushOn_80219230 as a bounded while loop over 0x21 entries with an early stop on key == -1 and __assert fallback.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The raw __assert was kept rather than converted to a macro; PR evidence does not show whether a macro form was attempted.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used short-circuit early-return control flow in fn_80201BE0 to avoid display when two gm_801A45E8 checks or Camera_8003010C are active.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: No review discussion explains whether alternate branch shapes were tested.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recovered grCastleParams field sizing from one f32 at x4 to two s16 fields x4/x6, then cast x4 to f32 at use site.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: The PR slice does not include additional data-layout proof beyond the diff and match report.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used M2C_FIELD for Corneria threshold access through grCn_804D69A0 at offset 0x38.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This is bridge code under typed-fields-over-pointer-math; the PR evidence does not prove the owning type for grCn_804D69A0.

review_feedback.feedback: decomp-dev[bot] reported six new 100.00% matches and +680 matched bytes for GALE01 from d64e258 to c66eb32.
No loaded review comments or reviews were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for cleanup of M2C_FIELD(grCn_804D69A0, f32*, 0x38) or a recovered Corneria parameter/data type.
Search later PRs for removal or justification of PAD_STACK(16) in fn_8020B4D8.
Search grpushon follow-ups for grPushOn_Lookup field naming or conversion of __assert("grpushon.c", 0x35D, "0") to an assert macro.
Search Old Yoshi follow-ups for semantic naming of grOldYoshi_Entry bitfields b0123/b4/b567 and grOy_JointPair fields x/y.
Search grcastle follow-ups for semantic naming or broader validation of grCastleParams x4/x6 split.

notes: Strong evidence for PR identity, changed files, function names, signatures, diff content, and bot-reported 100% matches.
The PR body claims ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0 for all functions; the bot report corroborates six new 100.00% matches but does not include full build logs.
No human review comments are loaded, so reviewer intent and requested cleanup cannot be inferred.
Some tactics remain weakly explained by PR evidence: M2C_FIELD in grcorneria, PAD_STACK in grzebesroute, and raw __assert in grpushon.
The PR body notes Claude Code generation; this is evidence about authorship/tooling note only, not proof that any specific shape was AI-selected.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered stage callbacks should update owning header prototypes
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2263 matched multiple stage functions and changed their headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to recovered signatures in the same PR, consistent with truthful-header practice.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Small typed GroundVars union arms can unblock stage callback matches
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2263 added grOldYoshi_Entry and grOldYoshi_GroundVars to types.h, added the oldyoshi union arm to Ground, and used it in fn_8020F2A8 for a matched Old Yoshi callback.

source_update_candidates.title: Track bridge tactics even when a function is reported 100% matched
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2263 shows a 100% matched stage-function batch that still includes M2C_FIELD and PAD_STACK in two recovered bodies. Curator may consider adding this as an example under matching-tactics-need-evidence or typed-fields-over-pointer-math, emphasizing that such tactics should remain narrow and be revisited when types/stack shape are understood.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR evidence supports the presence of narrow tactics in matched functions but does not by itself prove they are preferred style; route as a standards example candidate, not an accepted rule.

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/gr stage matching often touches paired .c/.h files plus shared gr/types.h
source_update_candidates.text: This PR matched six stage functions and changed implementation files, owning headers for recovered prototypes, and shared gr/types.h for GroundVars typing.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could help future path-scope lookup for stage decomp work; evidence is limited to this PR slice and should be curated as an example/path fact only if corroborated.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize that raw __assert is acceptable style from this PR alone; no review or standards evidence says a macro conversion was impossible.
Do not promote M2C_FIELD(grCn_804D69A0, f32*, 0x38) as final typed style; it is explicitly bridge-like under the loaded standards.
Do not infer semantic names for grOldYoshi_Entry bitfields, grPushOn_Lookup fields beyond key/value, or grCastleParams x4/x6 beyond what the diff names.
Do not claim objdiff was run manually; the loaded evidence contains PR-body fuzzy match claims and a bot report, not detailed local objdiff logs.
